data.title: Electric Royalties plans to raise C$4M in private placement
data.description:
• Electric Royalties (TSXV:ELEC:CA) entered into an agreement with Canaccord Genuity and Red Cloud Securities to act as agents, on a "best efforts" basis, in connection with a private placement of up to ~22.22M units at a price of C$0.18 per unit.
  • Gross proceeds expected to be ~C$4M.

  • Each unit will be comprised of one common share and one common share purchase warrant.

  • The net proceeds will be used to complete the acquisition of the 0.75% gross revenue royalty relating to the Punitaqui copper mine in Chile and for general corporate purposes.

  • The offering is expected to close on or about December 20, 2024.

data.title: Protara Therapeutics drops 6%, prices $100M offering
data.description:
• Protara Therapeutics (NASDAQ:TARA) priced its underwritten public offering of 13.69M shares at a price to the public of $6.25 per share and pre-funded warrants to purchase ~2.33M shares of common stock at a price of $6.249 per pre-funded warrant.
  • Underwriters have a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional ~2.4M shares.
  • The gross proceeds from the offering are expected to be approximately $100M.
  • The offering is expected to close on December 11, 2024.
  • Net proceeds received from the offering will be to fund the clinical development of TARA-002, as well as the development of other clinical programs. Protara (TARA) may also use the net proceeds from the offering for working capital and other general corporate purposes.
  • The stock price declined 6% on Monday during after mrket hours of trading.

data.title: Boeing restarts 737 MAX production a month after strike ends - report
data.description:
Boeing (NYSE:BA) has restarted the production of its 737 MAX aircraft last week, after almost a month of concluding the 53-day long strike involving 33,000 factory workers, Reuters reported on Tuesday, citing sources familiar with the matter.

This restart is critical for Boeing (BA) as it faces significant financial pressures and has around 4,200 orders for the 737 MAX jetliner. The production resumed on Friday, the report said.

The Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) had capped 737 MAX production at 38 planes per month due to safety concerns stemming from previous incidents, including a door panel malfunction that raised alarms about the jet's safety.

The FAA said last month that it will increase oversight of Boeing (BA), and its administrator, Mike Whitaker, stressed the importance of Boeing (BA) managing safety risks with the restart of 737 MAX production.

Last week, Whitaker said he would be shocked if it took less than several months for Boeing (BA) to reach the maximum 38, but he declined to say when FAA would allow the company to produce more than 38 in a month.

After a series of setbacks, including two fatal crashes, the COVID-19 epidemic, supply chain issues, increased regulatory scrutiny, and the most recent strike, Boeing's (BA) aspirations to raise 737 MAX production to a planned 56 aircraft per month have been thwarted.

data.title: DiamondRock Hospitality declares $0.03 quarterly dividend; stub dividend of $0.20

data.title: Invesco reports 4.8% increase in month-end assets under management
data.description:
• Invesco (NYSE:IVZ) Monday reported preliminary month-end assets under management of $1,856.5 billion, an increase of 4.8% versus previous month-end.
  • The firm delivered net long-term inflows of $11.0 billion in the month.
  • Non-management fee earning net inflows were $9.2 billion and money market net inflows were $14.9 billion, Invesco (IVZ) said.
  • AUM was positively impacted by favorable market returns which increased AUM by $52 billion.
  • Preliminary average total AUM for the quarter through November 30 was $1,805.4 billion, and preliminary average active AUM for the quarter through November 30 were $1,021.0 billion.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Germany's inflation rises to 2.2% in November, as expected
data.description:
• Inflation rate in Germany increased to 2.20% in November from 2% in October.

  • The Consumer Price Index decreased 0.20% in November over the previous month.

  • ETFs: (EWG), (GF), (FGM), (DAX), (FLGR) (DAX:IND)

  • Currency: (EUR:USD)
